producer.displayName: Codex CLI local task with scope drift
producer.trustBoundary: The producer reports useful work plus extra files. Reviewers must inspect normalized evidence, Boundary Map findings, and policy summaries before accepting the drift.

source.workspacePolicy: Use one dedicated worktree for the sample Issue and do not stage .codex-local/tasks/.

rawSignals.value: Codex added the requested reservation audit docs and a focused test, then also rewrote a payment settlement helper outside the sample Issue scope.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: This is producer text. It is evidence input, not approval or a block decision.

rawSignals.value: Changed file src/payments/settlement-retry.ts is outside the intended docs/tests/example scope.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: The normalizer keeps this visible; Boundary Map and reviewer disposition determine the next action.

rawSignals.value: Focused reservation audit unit test passed in the producer session.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: A focused test supports the intended docs/test change but does not justify the payment helper rewrite.

rawSignals.value: The producer did not explain why the payment settlement helper changed or provide evidence for that out-of-scope file.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Missing rationale is report-only evidence until policy or reviewer selects enforcement.

rawSignals.value: If the out-of-scope file were accepted as a payment or settlement change, reviewers would consider risk:high / enforce-assurance escalation.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Risk escalation is selected by policy and reviewer judgment, not by producer confidence.

changedFiles.role: intended documentation change from the sample Issue
focused test evidence for the intended documentation update
out-of-scope producer change that requires reviewer disposition

commands.evidenceUse: Focused behavior evidence for the intended docs/test scope.
Documentation consistency evidence for the intended scope.
Boundary drift should be supplied by the control plane before accepting the out-of-scope file.

claimsMentioned.rawAssertion: The primary docs/test changes are in the intended Issue scope, but one payment helper is outside scope and needs reviewer disposition.
claimsMentioned.controlPlaneBoundary: The out-of-scope changed file must remain unresolved until Boundary Map evidence and reviewer judgment are recorded.

claimsMentioned.rawAssertion: The agent finished the task, but the completion text does not approve the payment helper rewrite.
claimsMentioned.controlPlaneBoundary: Policy decision remains report-only or blocking according to risk labels and assurance mode.

expectedArtifacts.purpose: Normalize raw Codex CLI scope-drift output into report-only routing context.
Show changed-file and boundary drift as design-boundary evidence, not proof failure.
Surface scope drift as reviewer action and residual risk in the review surface.
Show normal-lane report-only behavior and strict high-risk blocking behavior without trusting producer text as approval.

knownGaps.summary: Out-of-scope changed files are reviewer evidence; they are not automatically proof failures or automatic blocks.
Boundary Map drift is report-only by default and escalates only when risk/high-assurance policy selects enforcement.

missingEvidence.summary: Command evidence is not complete: pnpm -s run context-pack:verify-boundary-map (not-run-in-producer-output)
Claim has no supporting evidence list: scope-drift-within-declared-boundary

unresolvedNotes: ACP-SCOPE-DRIFT-001: Out-of-scope changed files are reviewer evidence; they are not automatically proof failures or automatic blocks.
ACP-SCOPE-DRIFT-002: Boundary Map drift is report-only by default and escalates only when risk/high-assurance policy selects enforcement.

controlPlaneJudgment.reason: Report-only normalization summarizes raw producer output and routing gaps; it does not decide pass, proved, or approved status.
